summary.hook: Google's Gemma 4 drops open weights into a world where oil just spiked 11% and fear rules everything.

ai_news.title: Google Releases Gemma 4 Open Models
ai_news.body: Google launched Gemma 4 on April 2, 2026, calling it the most intelligent open model family it has produced. The release emphasizes advanced reasoning and agentic workflows with what Google describes as unprecedented intelligence-per-parameter efficiency. Hugging Face quickly stood up a Gemma 4 Playground on Spaces with ZeroGPU access, signaling immediate community adoption.

ai_news.title: OpenAI Acquires TBPN in Expansion Push
ai_news.body: OpenAI announced it has successfully acquired TBPN, a deal the company says will enhance its capabilities in AI and machine learning applications. Details on TBPN's technology stack and deal terms have not been disclosed. The acquisition follows OpenAI's record $122 billion fundraise and signals continued aggressive expansion beyond model development.

ai_news.title: Codex Gets Pay-as-You-Go Team Pricing
ai_news.body: OpenAI introduced flexible, usage-based pricing for its Codex coding platform, replacing fixed-tier plans for teams. The pay-as-you-go model is designed to lower the barrier for smaller developer teams and organizations that do not need always-on capacity. No specific per-token or per-request rates were disclosed in the announcement.

ai_news.title: April Prediction Markets Show Crowded Pipeline
ai_news.body: Manifold prediction markets now price Gemma 4 at 98.8% resolved for April, with five other models above 55%: MiniMax M3 (65%), Kimi K3 (63%), GPT-5.5 or variant (62%), DeepSeek V4 (58%), and Gemini 3.1 Flash (57%). Claude variants trail at 27% to 35%, while GPT-6 sits at just 10%. The data suggests April could rival March's nine-model output.

ai_news.title: Hugging Face Ships TRL v1.0 Training Library
ai_news.body: Hugging Face released TRL v1.0, marking a major overhaul of its post-training library for large language models. The team described the release as a shift from research codebase to production-grade library, built to keep pace with rapidly evolving fine-tuning and alignment techniques. TRL supports reinforcement learning from human feedback and related post-training workflows.

ai_news.title: GPT-5.4 Matched Gemini 3.1 Pro at Top
ai_news.body: A detailed March retrospective from WhatLLM confirmed GPT-5.4 (xhigh) scored 57.17 on the Intelligence Index, just 0.01 points behind Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview's 57.18. The report noted nine text models shipped in March, seven of them open-weight, with three using mixture-of-experts architectures. The near-tie at the top received surprisingly little industry attention.

ai_news.title: Pentagon Labels Anthropic a Supply-Chain Risk
ai_news.body: Multiple U.S. agencies began phasing out Claude models over a six-month transition after Anthropic refused to loosen restrictions on autonomous weapons use, according to WhatLLM's March roundup. Anthropic received a "supply-chain risk" designation, a label normally reserved for foreign adversaries. OpenAI moved quickly to secure a new Department of Defense agreement in the wake of the decision.

ai_news.title: Coder Raises $90M Series C From KKR
ai_news.body: Coder, the enterprise development environment platform, closed a $90 million Series C round led by KKR to advance secure enterprise AI development. The funding reflects growing investor appetite for infrastructure that governs how developers interact with AI coding tools inside corporate environments. No valuation was disclosed.

ai_news.title: March Shipped Seven Open-Weight Models in One Month
ai_news.body: Of the nine text models released in March 2026, seven were open-weight, continuing a decisive shift toward accessible model distribution. Alibaba's Qwen 3.5 series spanned 0.8B to 397B parameters across multiple MoE configurations, while the large variant scored 45 on the Intelligence Index. The open-weight dominance is reshaping the competitive landscape from the middle of the leaderboard upward.

ai_news.title: NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition Backs Open Frontier Models
ai_news.body: At GTC 2026 in mid-March, NVIDIA announced the Nemotron Coalition with partners including Mistral, Perplexity, and Cursor to develop open frontier models. Jensen Huang also revealed over $1 trillion in infrastructure orders in the pipeline and debuted the Vera Rubin AI platform, which claims roughly 10x training cost reduction for trillion-parameter models. New dedicated inference chips and an open Agent Toolkit were also unveiled.

world_news.title: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel and Gulf States
world_news.body: Iran launched a new wave of missile strikes targeting Israel and Gulf state energy infrastructure on April 3, as explosions were reported around Tehran and Isfahan from ongoing U.S. and Israeli counterstrikes. The attacks mark a significant escalation in the fifth day of open warfare, with President Trump claiming most targeted Iranian leaders are now dead and signaling further operations.

world_news.title: Oil Surges 11.4% to $111.54 on Hormuz Threat
world_news.body: U.S. crude closed at $111.54 per barrel on April 3, posting an 11.4% single-day gain, the largest since April 2020. The spike was driven by Iran's attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure and its tightening control over the Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly a fifth of global oil transits.

world_news.title: U.S. Sinks Iranian Warship Off Sri Lanka
world_news.body: The U.S. military sank the Iranian naval vessel IRIS Dena in the Indian Ocean off Sri Lanka's coast. Sri Lanka's navy recovered 87 bodies and rescued 32 survivors from the wreckage, marking one of the deadliest single naval engagements of the conflict so far.

world_news.title: Spain Refuses U.S. Base Access, Trump Threatens Trade
world_news.body: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez demanded an immediate end to the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran, calling it a "disaster," and denied the U.S. access to Spanish military bases. President Trump responded by threatening a full trade cutoff with Spain, opening a new rift within the Western alliance.

world_news.title: Anti-U.S. Protests Escalate Across Pakistan
world_news.body: Violent protests continued across Pakistan after U.S. Marines killed 10 demonstrators who stormed the American consulate in Karachi on Sunday. The State Department ordered the evacuation of consular staff from both Karachi and Lahore, citing deteriorating security conditions in the country.

world_news.title: Israel Passes Death Penalty for Palestinian Attackers
world_news.body: The Knesset voted 62 to 48 to enact a death penalty law targeting Palestinians convicted of intentionally killing Israelis to deny the state's existence. Far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir celebrated the passage with champagne. The law drew immediate international condemnation from human rights organizations.

world_news.title: Bangladesh Imposes Austerity Amid Fuel Crisis
world_news.body: Bangladesh announced sweeping energy austerity measures including shortened office hours, mall closures, a 30% cut to government fuel spending, and bans on decorative lighting. The country, which imports 95% of its fuel, is seeking $2.5 billion in emergency financing as the Iran conflict disrupts global energy supply chains.

world_news.title: U.S. Deploys Special Forces to Ecuador
world_news.body: The United States deployed special operations forces to Ecuador, expanding its military footprint in Latin America. The move comes as Washington juggles a widening Middle East conflict with ongoing security partnerships in the Western Hemisphere, though specific mission details remain undisclosed.
